meta.title: Palm Sunday / Jesus Washes His Disciples' Feet
meta.verseText: Don't do anything only to get ahead. Don't do it because you are proud. Instead, be humble. Value others more than yourselves.
meta.theme: Jesus showed us how to be humble

questions.text: What city was Jesus traveling toward in today's story?
questions.hint: It's the city where Passover was celebrated.
questions.explanation: Jesus and His disciples were traveling toward Jerusalem where people were gathering for Passover.

questions.text: What holiday were the people getting ready for when Jesus arrived?
questions.hint: This holiday remembers when God freed the Israelites from Egypt.
questions.explanation: The city was packed with people getting ready for Passover.

questions.text: What did the crowds wave as Jesus entered Jerusalem?
questions.hint: These come from a type of tree.
questions.explanation: The people took branches from palm trees and went out to meet Jesus.

questions.text: What did the crowds shout when Jesus entered Jerusalem?
questions.hint: It means 'Save us, we pray!'
questions.explanation: The crowds shouted 'Hosanna!' which means 'Save us, we pray!'

questions.text: What animal did Jesus ride into Jerusalem?
questions.hint: It's a humble animal, not one a powerful king would usually ride.
questions.explanation: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it as He entered Jerusalem, fulfilling Scripture.

questions.text: True or False: Jesus rode into Jerusalem on a big white horse.
questions.hint: Think about what kind of animal showed humility.
questions.explanation: Jesus rode a young donkey, not a horse. This showed His humility because a donkey was a humble animal.

questions.text: What did Jesus do for His disciples at the special meal?
questions.hint: This was a job usually done by a servant.
questions.explanation: At the special meal, Jesus knelt down and washed His disciples' feet, doing the job of a servant.

questions.text: What does 'Hosanna' mean?
questions.hint: The people were asking for help.
questions.explanation: Hosanna means 'Save us, we pray!' The crowds were calling out to Jesus for salvation.

questions.text: Why did Jesus ride a donkey instead of a horse?
options: To show He was a humble king
questions.hint: Think about today's Bottom Line.
questions.explanation: Jesus chose a humble donkey to show that He came as a humble, servant king, not a powerful warrior king.

questions.text: True or False: Washing someone's feet was considered a very important and honorable job.
questions.hint: Think about who usually did this job.
questions.explanation: Washing feet was actually a lowly servant's job. That's what made it so amazing that Jesus, the King, chose to do it.

questions.text: What is this week's Bottom Line?
options: Jesus showed us how to be brave
Jesus showed us how to be humble
Jesus showed us how to be strong
Jesus showed us how to be smart
questions.hint: It's about putting others first.
questions.explanation: The Bottom Line is 'Jesus showed us how to be humble.' Humility means putting others first.

questions.text: What book of the Bible is today's story found in?
questions.hint: This book was written by one of Jesus' closest friends.
questions.explanation: Today's story comes from the book of John, chapters 12 and 13.

questions.text: What does it mean to be humble?
options: Putting others first instead of yourself
questions.hint: Think about what Jesus did for His friends.
questions.explanation: Being humble means putting others first instead of trying to be the most important. Jesus showed this by serving His disciples.

questions.text: Jesus showed us that real greatness comes from being _____ and willing to help others.
questions.hint: Think about how Jesus treated His friends.
questions.explanation: Jesus showed that real greatness comes from being kind, gentle, and willing to help others, even when it's hard.

questions.text: True or False: Jesus came to Jerusalem to show off His power.
questions.hint: Remember why Jesus rode a donkey.
questions.explanation: Jesus didn't come to show off His power. He came to show love and to serve others.

questions.text: Who helps us to be humble like Jesus?
questions.hint: This helper lives inside every believer.
questions.explanation: The Holy Spirit lives inside us and helps us to be humble and to serve others like Jesus did.

questions.text: The memory verse for this month is found in _____.
questions.hint: It's a letter Paul wrote to a church.
questions.explanation: The monthly memory verse is Philippians 2:3, which tells us to be humble and value others more than ourselves.

questions.text: According to the memory verse, we should value others _____ than ourselves.
questions.hint: It's the opposite of being selfish.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 says to 'value others more than yourselves,' which is what humility looks like.

questions.text: What miracle had Jesus done for His friend Lazarus?
questions.hint: This was one of Jesus' most amazing miracles.
questions.explanation: Jesus had brought His friend Lazarus back to life, which was one reason the crowds were so excited to see Him.

questions.text: True or False: The crowds were bored when Jesus arrived in Jerusalem.
questions.hint: Think about how you'd react if you saw someone who could do miracles!
questions.explanation: The crowds were not bored at all! They cheered, waved palm branches, and shouted 'Hosanna!' when Jesus arrived.

questions.text: What kind of king did the people expect to come?
questions.hint: Jesus surprised everyone by being different.
questions.explanation: People expected a powerful king who would use force, but Jesus came as a humble servant king riding a donkey instead of a war horse.

questions.text: This month's theme is called _____.
questions.hint: It means doing things differently from what the world expects.
questions.explanation: The monthly theme is 'Upside Down' because living like Jesus means doing things the opposite of what the world expects.

questions.text: Which of these is a way you can be humble like Jesus?
options: Always making sure you go first
Letting a friend pick the game to play
questions.hint: Think about putting others first.
questions.explanation: Letting a friend choose shows humility because you're putting their wishes ahead of your own, just like Jesus put others first.

questions.text: If your friend is struggling to carry their books, what would a humble person do?
options: Offer to help carry some books
questions.hint: What would Jesus do?
questions.explanation: A humble person helps others, just like Jesus served His disciples by washing their feet. Offering to help carry books puts your friend's needs first.

questions.text: At recess, everyone wants to be team captain. How can you show humility?
options: Let someone else be captain this time
Tell the teacher it's not fair
questions.hint: Remember, humility means putting others first.
questions.explanation: Letting someone else have a turn shows humility. Jesus could have demanded to be served, but He chose to serve instead.

questions.text: Why is it sometimes hard to be humble?
options: Because the world tells us to get ahead and be important
Because God doesn't want us to be humble
questions.hint: Think about what the lesson said about the world's message.
questions.explanation: The world tells us we should always try to win and be the best, which makes humility hard. But Jesus showed us a better way.

questions.text: True or False: Only adults need to practice being humble.
questions.hint: Does Jesus want ALL His followers to be humble?
questions.explanation: Everyone, including kids, can and should practice humility. Jesus wants all of His followers to put others first.

questions.text: When we are humble and serve others, we are showing the world what _____ is like.
questions.hint: We are reflecting someone very important.
questions.explanation: Every time we are humble, we show the world what Jesus is like. We represent Him to everyone around us.

questions.text: Your little sibling wants to play a game you think is boring. What's the humble choice?
options: Say no and play your own game
Tell them their game is dumb
Hide so they can't find you
questions.hint: What would Jesus do for someone He loves?
questions.explanation: Playing their game cheerfully puts their joy ahead of your own preferences, just like Jesus put others ahead of Himself.

questions.text: According to the lesson, Jesus is the King of the entire _____.
questions.hint: It's bigger than a country or a planet!
questions.explanation: Jesus is the King of the entire universe, yet He chose to serve others humbly instead of demanding to be served.

questions.text: What did the Scripture say about the City of Zion when Jesus arrived?
questions.hint: The Scripture had a comforting message.
questions.explanation: John 12:15 says 'City of Zion, do not be afraid. See, your king is coming. He is sitting on a donkey's colt.'

questions.text: True or False: Jesus washed His disciples' feet to show that serving others is important.
questions.hint: This was a powerful example of humility.
questions.explanation: True! Jesus washed His disciples' feet to show them that serving others is an important part of following God. He led by example.

questions.text: You got the highest score on a test. How can you be humble about it?
options: Tell everyone how smart you are
Thank God and encourage friends who struggled
questions.hint: Humility doesn't mean hiding your success—it means not bragging.
questions.explanation: Being humble means not bragging. You can be grateful and also encourage others, just like Jesus lifted people up instead of putting them down.

questions.text: In today's story, Jesus chose to _____ instead of being served.
questions.hint: He did the opposite of what a king normally does.
questions.explanation: Jesus chose to serve instead of being served. He washed His disciples' feet, showing that true greatness comes from serving others.

questions.text: What were some of the amazing things Jesus had done that made the crowds so excited?
options: He made sick people well and calmed storms
He built houses and planted gardens
He won races and played games
He wrote books and sang songs
questions.hint: Think about the miracles mentioned in the story.
questions.explanation: The crowds were excited because Jesus had healed the sick, calmed storms, and even raised Lazarus from the dead.

questions.text: According to the memory verse, we shouldn't do things only to get _____.
questions.hint: It's about selfish ambition.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 says 'Don't do anything only to get ahead' — we should not act out of selfish ambition but out of humility.

questions.text: A new kid sits alone at lunch. What's the humble, Jesus-like thing to do?
options: Sit with them and introduce yourself
Wait for someone else to help
questions.hint: Jesus always noticed people who were left out.
questions.explanation: Sitting with the new kid and being friendly puts their needs first, just like Jesus always noticed and cared for people who were overlooked.

questions.text: True or False: Being humble means you can never feel good about yourself.
questions.hint: Humility isn't about thinking less of yourself.
questions.explanation: False! Being humble doesn't mean feeling bad about yourself. It means valuing others and putting their needs alongside your own, just like Jesus did.

questions.text: What did the people call Jesus as He entered Jerusalem?
questions.hint: They recognized Him as their ruler.
questions.explanation: The crowds shouted 'Blessed is the King of Israel!' recognizing Jesus as their King as He entered Jerusalem.

questions.text: How does Jesus help us be humble every day?
options: He goes with us every minute and helps through the Holy Spirit
He makes us forget about ourselves
He takes away all our talents
questions.hint: We have a helper who is always with us.
questions.explanation: Jesus goes with us every minute of every day through the Holy Spirit, who lives inside believers and helps us to be humble and serve others.

termPairs.definition: Putting others first instead of yourself
What the crowds waved when Jesus arrived
The humble animal Jesus rode into Jerusalem
The city Jesus entered on Palm Sunday
The holiday the people were celebrating
The servant's job Jesus did for His disciples
The memory verse about being humble
The friend Jesus brought back to life
The helper who lives inside us and helps us be humble
The day Jesus entered Jerusalem as crowds cheered
Someone who puts others' needs first

keyWords.hint: Putting others first instead of yourself
The crowd shouted this word meaning 'Save us!'
The city Jesus entered on a donkey
The animal Jesus rode instead of a horse
Jesus took on this role by washing feet
The type of branches the crowds waved
Jesus brought this friend back to life
Jesus' closest followers whose feet He washed
Lifting our voices and serving others for God
Jesus showed this comes from kindness, not power
The Bible book where the memory verse is found
Jesus wants us to make the world look like His _____
The Holy _____ helps us to be humble

story.summary: As Jesus traveled toward Jerusalem for Passover, huge crowds gathered to welcome Him. They waved palm branches and shouted 'Hosanna!' calling Him the King of Israel. But Jesus surprised everyone by riding in on a humble donkey instead of a powerful horse. Later, at a special meal with His disciples, Jesus did something even more surprising—He knelt down and washed their feet, doing the job of a lowly servant. Jesus, the King of the universe, showed us that true greatness comes from being humble and putting others first.

scenes.title: The Journey to Jerusalem
scenes.description: Jesus and His disciples traveled toward Jerusalem. The city was packed with people getting ready for Passover. Everyone had heard about Jesus—how He healed the sick, calmed storms, and even brought Lazarus back to life. Excitement was building as word spread that Jesus was coming.

scenes.title: The Triumphal Entry
scenes.description: As Jesus got close to the city, crowds gathered on the road. They took branches from palm trees and went out to meet Him. They shouted 'Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessed is the King of Israel!' The people cheered and celebrated His arrival.

scenes.title: The Humble King on a Donkey
scenes.description: Instead of riding a powerful war horse like people expected a king to ride, Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it. This fulfilled what Scripture had promised. Jesus showed that He was a different kind of King—a humble King who came to serve, not to show off His power.

scenes.title: Jesus Washes His Disciples' Feet
scenes.description: At a special meal with His disciples, Jesus did something no one expected. The King of the universe knelt down and washed His friends' feet—a job that was usually done by the lowest servant. Jesus showed His disciples that real greatness comes from being kind, gentle, and willing to help others.

scenes.title: Living Humbly Like Jesus
scenes.description: Jesus showed us that being humble means putting others first. He is the King of the entire universe, but He chose to serve instead of being served. Through the Holy Spirit, Jesus helps us to be humble every day. Every time we put others first, we show the world what Jesus is like.
